This week @graphprotocol turned every Subgraph on their network into an x402 endpoint.\n\nUnleash your agents onto thousands of verified onchain data sources and pay-per-query in @USDC on @base, all with zero API keys required.

������ Here’s everything announced this week:

→ Agentic․market went live: the homepage of the agentic economy where humans and agents can discover and integrate APIs ready to use with x402.

→ @NiumGlobal integrated our stablecoin payment rails, funding cross-border payouts in USDC across 190+ countries with no prefunding and or wire delays.

→ @graphprotocol Gateway now settles Subgraph queries in USDC on @base via x402 with thousands of onchain data sources and zero API keys required.

→ @Alchemy AgentPay turns any API into an x402-ready endpoint with zero code changes required.

→ @Nick_Prince12 showed how he found @ExaAILabs search endpoint on agentic․market and used it to pull together research in minutes for $0.01 via x402 without any API keys.

→ @Jnix2007 announced major updates to CDP Wallets including gas sponsorship for @solana and delegated signing.

Coming up next week:

→ Fireside Talk at our Payments Open House in SF on April 28.

→ @programmer takes the stage with @alchemy at CoBuild NYC on April 28 to talk about agentic commerce.

• bankr ($BNKR): the leader in agentic infrastructure and portfolio management.

• giza agents (GIZA): focused on zero-knowledge proofs for agents.
• xagent (XAGE): cross-platform agent integration.

remember there are still people giving their agents api keys

Wtf is Open Agentic Commerce

You are an API provider. You want to give people access to your API in chatgpt.

You need to call sama, get an enterprise agreement, set up api keys. It sucks. You don't have that kinda clout. And you are afraid of losing to competitors.

Agents are the future you tell yourself (you are right). If Agents can not call my API I'm fucked you tell yourself (you are right again).

This is where OAC will safe you. Put your api behind x402 or mpp. Will take you less than a day. I promise.

Now any agent with a wallet can call your api! No api keys. No signups. Nothing. Your agent does a micro payment every time it calls the API. Just like that.

You didn't talk to anyone. You didn't sign any agreement.

Welcome to Open Agentic Commerce

So I wouldn’t write this off as “micropayments with crypto.” 

The bigger shift is that agents need a payment primitive native to how they operate: to enable web/tool discovery, evaluation/reasoning, payment, and access.  

This is critical for agentic ux, especially for autonomous research.

A few things that might be helpful conceptually to note here:

1. The point of x402 is to drive cross-industry collaboration around a standard way for machines to discover terms, pay, and access content without needing a human subscription or billing flow in the loop.  This initiative is owned and supported by Linux, and associate, general, premier participants.

2. x402 is payment-rail agnostic. Crypto is one rail, but the protocol is designed to support multiple payment networks, including credit cards and other traditional payment methods.

3. Stablecoins are especially useful for request-time access to APIs, content, and digital services because they give agents a fast, programmable, global way to pay per request without account setup, invoicing, or platform-specific integrations.

������ Under the Hood: How x402 turns APIs into products

Most APIs today aren’t products.

They’re just… endpoints.

x402 flips this completely. https://t.co/g6zpAvJQkm

Instantly. That’s what scales.
